billwise-dashboard.title: BillWise Dashboard
billwise-dashboard.description: Fullstack personal finance platform with budgets, analytics, and recurring transactions.
billwise-dashboard.longDescription: A complete rewrite of Account Management using Next.js 16 App Router, Prisma, and Supabase Auth. Replaces the SPA + Express architecture with Server Components and Server Actions, adding budgets, analytics, notifications, recurrences, and an admin area.

features.auth: Full authentication with Supabase Auth: sign-up, login, password reset, and email verification
features.dashboard: Financial dashboard with monthly summary and revenue vs. expenses chart
features.budgets: Category budgets with progress bars and visual alerts when limits are reached
features.analytics: Analytics with financial evolution over time and spending breakdown by category
features.recurrences: Recurring billing rules with automatic monthly account generation via cron job
features.notifications: Notification center with filters by type: overdue, due soon, budget exceeded
features.admin: Admin area with user overview, protected by RBAC (admin role)

architecture.appRouter: Next.js App Router with Server Components — eliminates the API layer for most operations
architecture.serverActions: Server Actions for all mutations, no manual API routes needed
architecture.prisma: Prisma ORM with versioned migrations, typed queries, and atomic transactions
architecture.rbac: RBAC with user and admin roles, server-side guards on every route and action
architecture.cron: Cron jobs via API route for generating recurrences and processing due dates

outcome.context: Account Management v1 was a React + Vite SPA with a serverless Express backend. It worked well as a reference project, but had clear limitations: no ORM, no server-side rendering, basic features, and a split frontend/backend architecture.
outcome.solution: A full rewrite using Next.js 16 App Router, Prisma 7, and Supabase Auth. The integration of Server Components and Server Actions eliminated the Express layer for most operations, while Prisma brought typed queries and versioned migrations. The scope was expanded with budgets, analytics, notifications, recurrences, and an admin area.
outcome.result: The project is in active development with a deployment coming soon. The new architecture is significantly more maintainable, testable, and scalable than v1.

consulta-nfe-cte.title: NF-e / CT-e Query Tool
consulta-nfe-cte.description: Desktop application for querying NF-e/CT-e associations and uploading delivery receipts via internal API, without a browser.
consulta-nfe-cte.longDescription: Built for internal use at a logistics company, the system automates NF-e → CT-e association lookups, renames receipt images using OCR, and performs batch uploads — all from a standalone executable generated with PyInstaller.

features.login: Automatic login with email and password, no browser required
features.search: NF-e range search with status table (With CT-e / Without CT-e / Not registered)
features.ocr: OCR-based NF-e number detection in images with automatic file renaming
features.upload: Batch upload of receipt images, skipping NF-es not in PENDING status
features.excel: Results export to Excel and restorable query history

architecture.threads: Background worker threads for OCR, renaming and upload (UI never blocks)
architecture.keyring: Credentials securely stored via Windows Credential Manager (keyring)
architecture.env: Environment variable configuration (.env) for portability across companies
architecture.build: Distributable build with PyInstaller generating a standalone .exe

account-management.title: Account Management — v1
account-management.description: Version 1 of the financial management system. React + Vite SPA with serverless Express backend and JWT authentication.
account-management.longDescription: Fullstack project with JWT authentication, complete accounts CRUD, financial Dashboard, and layered architecture. Served as the foundation for BillWise Dashboard (v2), a full rewrite using Next.js 16, Prisma, and Supabase Auth.

features.crud: Accounts CRUD with pagination and filters

architecture.context: Global state management with Context API

outcome.context: I worked with Power BI and Excel for data tracking before building this solution. The goal was to go beyond ready-made tools and build a fully coded system — using real household expenses as a concrete scenario to apply the technologies I was learning.
outcome.solution: I developed a Fullstack application focused on financial data visualization and management, building custom Dashboard components without relying on external UI libraries. The architecture prioritized clarity, reusability, and performance.
outcome.result: After publishing the demo, I made the system available for family members to collect real feedback. The experience exposed the limitations of the SPA + Express architecture, which motivated the full rewrite into BillWise Dashboard (v2) using Next.js 16, Prisma, and Supabase Auth.
